headlines.title: Trump doesn’t rule out giving Jan. 6 rioters who attacked police ‘anti-weaponization’ fund payouts
headlines.description: President Donald Trump didn’t rule out the government’s paying people who were charged with assaulting police officers during the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.

headlines.title: Federal lawsuit aims to stop UFC event on the White House South Lawn
headlines.description: Plaintiffs allege the June 14 event violates National Park Service regulations and benefits Trump and his allies.

headlines.title: Nithya Raman leaps past Spencer Pratt in tight race to make L.A. mayoral runoff
headlines.description: Los Angeles City Council member Nithya Raman, a Democrat, has taken a narrow lead for second place over former reality TV star Spencer Pratt, a registered Republican, in the race to take on Mayor Karen Bass in a runoff this fall.

headlines.title: Scott Pelley Speaks Out on 'Political Influence' at CBS News
headlines.description: Veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley called CBS leadership as incompetent and inexperienced in an emotional interview after he was fired from the storied broadcast. Pelley alleges editor-in-chief Bari Weiss directed his team to make ICE protestors look more violent, saying, “There was a thumb on the scale for the president’s version of events that I felt was a level of political influence that I have never seen in 37 years at CBS News.” The three remaining correspondents, Le

headlines.title: Spencer Pratt Drops to Third Place in LA Mayor’s Race
headlines.description: Votes are still being counted in the closely-watched primary races in California, including for mayor of Los Angeles. Conservative reality TV star Spencer Pratt has dropped to third place behind current democratic Mayor Karen Bass, who is heading to the runoff, and progressive city councilwoman Nithya Raman. NBC’s Liz Kreutz reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Why Did Trump Storm Out of Meet the Press Interview on NBC?
headlines.description: President Donald Trump abruptly ended his weekend interview with Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker after he was pressed for evidence to back up his baseless claims of election fraud in California’s primary races. The president also defended his administration’s controversial $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Read the transcript: President Donald Trump interviewed by NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’ moderator Kristen Welker
headlines.description: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.

headlines.title: Fact-checking Trump’s interview with NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’
headlines.description: Digging into some of the president’s claims about the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.

headlines.title: Send your questions for Steve Kornacki
headlines.description: NBC News’ chief data analyst Steve Kornacki will answer NBC News subscriber questions about the midterm elections and more in a live Q&A on Tuesday at 12 p.m. ET.

headlines.title: Mamdani makes ‘high-risk, high-reward’ move to reshape New York’s voice in Washington
headlines.description: The first-year mayor has endorsed two congressional candidates challenging incumbents.
